notes: Phase 3 first-line trial of abivertinib (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib in patients with sensitizing EGFR mutations (Ex19del or L858R). Prior EGFR TKI excluded.

trial_name: D-0316 in T790M+ EGFR NSCLC after 1st/2nd-gen TKI
notes: Phase 2 of D-0316 (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) in T790M+ NSCLC after progression on 1st/2nd-gen EGFR TKI. Prior 3rd-gen TKI excluded.
MELROSE: phase 2 first-line osimertinib in treatment-naive advanced EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Ex19del/L858R). Studies acquired resistance mechanisms.
Phase 3 of BPI-7711 (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib first-line. Common sensitizing EGFR mut required; T790M-positive primary excluded; exon 20 insertions excluded.
Randomized phase 2 of osimertinib + ramucirumab vs osimertinib alone, TKI-naive EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Previous chemo/IO allowed; prior EGFR TKI and prior anti-VEGF excluded.
Phase 2 dual-cohort lorlatinib monotherapy in ALK+ NSCLC after prior ALK inhibitor (Cohort 1: post-crizotinib only; Cohort 2: post 2G ALK TKI).
Phase 3 pemetrexed + platinum +/- toripalimab in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after EGFR-TKI failure (post-1st/2nd-gen without T790M, or post-osimertinib regardless of T790M status).

INSIGHT 2: tepotinib + osimertinib in MET-amplified, EGFR-mutant NSCLC after acquired resistance to prior osimertinib. Experimental arm is MET TKI + EGFR TKI; closest canonical class is 3rd-gen + chemo, but actually MET+EGFR-TKI; tagged as EGFR TKI 3rd-gen + chemotherapy as nearest match. May be considered partly out-of-scope (MET-amplified subset) but core eligibility is EGFR-mutant post-osimertinib.

ORCHARD: biomarker-directed phase 2 platform after first-line osimertinib progression. Multiple modules (Osi+savolitinib, Osi+gefitinib, Osi+necitumumab, carbo/pem/durva, Osi+alectinib, Osi+selpercatinib, etc.). Heterogeneous experimental arms; tagged 'investigational (other)' to reflect platform design.
Phase 1/2 of sunvozertinib (DZD9008) in EGFR or HER2 mutant NSCLC. Primary target population is EGFR Ex20ins, with HER2-mutant cohorts (HER2 out-of-scope). Mixed cohort; flag partially out-of-scope.

Out of scope (partial): SHP2 inhibitor RMC-4630 + cobimetinib arm enrolls KRAS/BRAF/NF1 (out-of-scope per protocol). RMC-4630 + osimertinib arm enrolls EGFR-mutant post-osimertinib NSCLC, which is in-scope. Mixed-scope; tagged investigational (other).

Phase 3 atezolizumab + carbo + paclitaxel + bev vs pemetrexed + platinum, in EGFR or ALK-driven NSCLC after prior TKI (post-osimertinib for T790M+; post-ALK TKI). IMpower150-style regimen; experimental arm class is IO + anti-VEGF + chemo; closest canonical is Anti-VEGF + chemotherapy.
Single-arm phase 2 of dacomitinib (2G EGFR TKI) with/without dose titration, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
Phase 3 gefitinib + anlotinib (anti-angiogenic TKI) vs gefitinib + placebo, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Closest canonical class is Bevacizumab + EGFR TKI (anti-angiogenic + EGFR TKI category).
FLAURA2: phase 3 osimertinib +/- platinum/pemetrexed first-line in EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Ex19del/L858R).
REZILIENT1: phase 1/2 CLN-081 (zipalertinib) in EGFR Ex20ins NSCLC post-platinum chemo (and prior approved Ex20ins TKI in Module C).
Phase 3 TAK-788 (mobocertinib) vs platinum chemo first-line in EGFR Ex20ins NSCLC.
Phase 3 ASK120067 (rezivertinib, 3G EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib, first-line EGFRm NSCLC.
Randomized phase 3 osimertinib +/- bevacizumab first-line in metastatic EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
Phase 2 osimertinib + aspirin vs osimertinib alone, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Aspirin is investigational add-on; no clean canonical class — tagged investigational (other).

trial_name: SAF-189s / foritinib in ALK or ROS1 NSCLC
notes: Out of scope (partial): SAF-189s (foritinib) phase 1/2 in ALK-positive (in-scope) and ROS1-positive (OUT-of-scope) NSCLC; mixed cohorts. Includes prior ALK/ROS1 TKI-treated and naive patients.
Phase 3 SH-1028 (3G EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib first-line, EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
LASER301: phase 3 lazertinib (3G EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib first-line, EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

Out of scope: SKYSCRAPER-01 enrolls PD-L1-high, EGFR/ALK-negative driver-negative NSCLC. No oncogenic driver — out of scope for v3 EGFR/ALK NSCLC corpus. Drug class: tiragolumab + atezolizumab, closest canonical is PD-1/PD-L1 monotherapy (with anti-TIGIT).

OCELOT: phase 2 sequential osimertinib first-line -> chemo at progression -> osimertinib rechallenge third-line in EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Atypical EGFR mutations allowed; exon 20 insertions excluded. Drug class scored as 3G EGFR TKI as it's the first-line and sentinel intervention.

trial_name: Gefitinib + anlotinib in uncleared ctDNA EGFRm after gefitinib
notes: Phase 2: addition of anlotinib (anti-angiogenic) to ongoing first-line gefitinib in NSCLC with uncleared ctDNA EGFR-mut after gefitinib. Treatment intensification on gefitinib; closest canonical class is Bevacizumab + EGFR TKI.

Out of scope: Stage II/IIIA resectable NSCLC neoadjuvant/adjuvant trial; explicitly excludes known EGFR mutation or ALK translocation. Early-stage perioperative setting, not metastatic. Out of scope for v3 metastatic EGFR/ALK corpus.
Phase 2 osimertinib vs osimertinib + carbo/pemetrexed in EGFR-mutant NSCLC with detectable EGFR cfDNA after osimertinib initiation (early intensification).
Phase 2 gefitinib +/- bevacizumab specifically in EGFR L858R-mutant NSCLC (first-line).

Out of scope: CONTACT-01 atezolizumab + cabozantinib vs docetaxel in metastatic NSCLC after IO + platinum chemo; explicitly excludes EGFR sensitizing mutations and ALK fusions (driver-negative). Out of scope for v3 EGFR/ALK corpus.
MARIPOSA: phase 3 amivantamab + lazertinib vs osimertinib vs lazertinib monotherapy, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Ex19del/L858R).

Out of scope: ROME is a tumor-agnostic precision oncology basket. Explicitly excludes EGFR-mut and ALK-translocation NSCLC (and other 'well-established actionable targets with approved drugs'). Pan-tumor / actionable-target-not-actionable design — out of scope for v3 EGFR/ALK NSCLC corpus.
APPEAL: phase 2 almonertinib (aumolertinib, 3G EGFR TKI) +/- pemetrexed/carboplatin in T790M+ NSCLC after 1st/2nd-gen EGFR TKI.
HERTHENA-Lung01: phase 2 patritumab deruxtecan in EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Ex19del/L858R) after prior osimertinib AND prior platinum chemotherapy.
Out of scope: DESTINY-LUNG02 enrolls HER2-mutated NSCLC; explicitly excludes EGFR, ALK, ROS1, BRAF, MET, RET, NTRK drivers. HER2-mutant NSCLC out of scope for v3.

trial_name: TOP (osimertinib +/- chemo in EGFR/TP53 co-mut NSCLC)
notes: TOP: phase 3 osimertinib +/- carbo/pemetrexed in untreated advanced non-squamous NSCLC with concurrent EGFR and TP53 mutations.

Multi-cohort: Cohort A driver-negative post-PD-1/L1 (out of scope), Cohort B EGFR/ALK post-TKI (in-scope). IBI318 (bispecific PD-1/PD-L1) + lenvatinib. Mixed scope; cohort B in-scope post-osimertinib/post-ALK-TKI.
SAINT: phase 3 anlotinib + icotinib vs icotinib in untreated EGFR concomitant-mutation NSCLC. Anti-angiogenic TKI + EGFR TKI; closest canonical class is Bevacizumab + EGFR TKI.
CAPLAND: randomised phase 2 osimertinib->dacomitinib vs dacomitinib->osimertinib sequencing in EGFR-mut NSCLC. First-line arm is 3G EGFR TKI.
ATTACK: phase 3 high-dose almonertinib vs osimertinib second-line in EGFR-mut NSCLC with brain metastases (post 1st/2nd-gen EGFR TKI).

Phase 3 almonertinib maintenance after definitive chemoradiation in Stage III unresectable EGFR-mut NSCLC. LAURA-like design; non-metastatic locally-advanced setting (partially out-of-scope for metastatic-only DAG, but EGFR-mut/post-CRT is a clearly defined NSCLC node).

trial_name: Almonertinib vs platinum chemo (uncommon EGFR first-line)
notes: Phase 3 almonertinib vs platinum chemo first-line in NSCLC with uncommon EGFR mutations (L861Q, G719X, or S768I).
MARIPOSA-2: phase 3 amivantamab + lazertinib + carbo/pemetrexed (or amivantamab + carbo/pem) vs carbo/pem in EGFR-mut NSCLC after osimertinib failure.
CLASSIC5: phase 2 chemo +/- ICI +/- bev in ALK-rearranged NSCLC after first-line alectinib failure. Tested role of ICI post-ALK TKI.

Phase 2 surufatinib + toripalimab + chemo in NSCLC: wild-type cohort (driver-negative first-line, partially out-of-scope) and EGFR T790M cohort post-osimertinib (in-scope). Mixed scope; tagged Anti-VEGF + chemotherapy (surufatinib is anti-angiogenic TKI).
